CREATE TABLE blobs (id INT, data BLOB);
SELECT id, length(data) + 10 AS adjusted_length FROM blobs;
SELECT id, (SELECT length(data) FROM blobs AS b WHERE b.id = blobs.id) AS blob_length FROM blobs;
SELECT b1.id, length(b1.data) + length(b2.data) AS total_length FROM blobs AS b1 JOIN blobs AS b2 ON b1.id < b2.id;
SELECT id, CASE WHEN length(data) > 4 THEN 'large' ELSE 'small' END AS size_category FROM blobs;
SELECT id, length(data) FROM blobs WHERE length(data) = (SELECT MAX(length(data)) FROM blobs);
SELECT id FROM blobs WHERE length(data) = (SELECT MIN(length(data)) FROM blobs);
CREATE TABLE additional_data (blob_id INT, info VARCHAR(255), FOREIGN KEY (blob_id) REFERENCES blobs(id));
SELECT b.id, length(b.data), ad.info FROM blobs AS b INNER JOIN additional_data AS ad ON b.id = ad.blob_id;
SELECT id FROM blobs WHERE length(data) <= 4 UNION SELECT id FROM blobs WHERE length(data) = 6;
